title: Review: Eval geometric semantics consistency (21 suggestions from task 224)
analysis: The evaluator in reify-expr handles component-wise arithmetic only for Value::Tensor, but Value::Point and Value::Vector are distinct enum variants (created by stdlib constructors point2/point3/vec2/vec3). When Point/Vector values flow into eval_add/eval_sub/eval_mul/eval_div/neg_value, they hit the catch-all `_ => Value::Undef` arm. This task adds proper Point/Vector handling with correct affine geometry rules (Point+Point=Undef, -Point=Undef, Point-Point=Vector, Point+Vector=Point, etc.), extracts DRY helpers to eliminate the collect/undef-check/wrap pattern duplication, adds missing test coverage, and fixes minor robustness issues. The escalation triage (esc-224-8) accepted 11 items for implementation and deferred 3 (early-exit optimization, arrayvec, empty container dimension). The existing code uses Value::Tensor in all point_vector_eval_tests — tests must be added/updated to use Value::Point/Value::Vector directly to exercise the new arms.

prerequisites.description: Extract componentwise_binop helper from existing Tensor arms in eval_add/eval_sub. Signature: fn componentwise_binop(a: &[Value], b: &[Value], op: fn(&Value, &Value) -> Value, wrap: fn(Vec<Value>) -> Value) -> Value. Refactor existing Tensor add/sub arms to use it. Also extract scale_components helper for scalar*components pattern used by eval_mul/eval_div/neg_value. Run existing tests to verify no regression.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Point/Vector addition: Vector+Vector→Vector, Point+Vector→Point, Vector+Point→Point, Point+Point→Undef (value-level, using Value::Point/Value::Vector directly). Add to point_vector_eval_tests.rs.

Add Point/Vector arms to eval_add using componentwise_binop helper: (Point,Vector)|(Vector,Point)→componentwise add→Point, (Vector,Vector)→componentwise add→Vector, (Point,Point)→Undef. Pass step-1 tests.

Write failing tests for Point/Vector subtraction: Point-Point→Vector (displacement), Point-Vector→Point, Vector-Vector→Vector, Vector-Point→Undef. Also replace the duplicate point3_length_sub test with a Point2-Point2 case.

Add Point/Vector arms to eval_sub: (Point,Point)→componentwise sub→Vector, (Point,Vector)→componentwise sub→Point, (Vector,Vector)→componentwise sub→Vector. Vector-Point falls through to Undef. Pass step-3 tests.
Write failing tests for Point/Vector negation: -Vector3→Vector3 (all components negated), -Point3→Undef (affine rule: points cannot be negated per spec 3.3.1).
Add Point/Vector arms to neg_value in eval_unop: Value::Vector→negate components→Vector (using map helper), Value::Point→Undef with comment citing affine space invariant. Pass step-5 tests.

Write failing tests for scalar*Point/Vector: Scalar*Vector→Vector, Vector*Scalar→Vector (commutative), Scalar*Point→Point (pragmatic deviation from strict affine rules, needed for interpolation), Int*Vector→Vector, Real*Point→Point.

Add Point/Vector arms to eval_mul using scale_components helper: (Point,scalar)|(scalar,Point)→scale→Point, (Vector,scalar)|(scalar,Vector)→scale→Vector. Add comment documenting scalar*Point as intentional pragmatic deviation from strict affine rules (needed for weighted interpolation/barycentric coordinates). Pass step-7 tests.
Write failing tests for Point/Vector division: Vector/Scalar→Vector, Vector/Real→Vector, Point/Scalar→Point (pragmatic deviation), Vector/Int→Vector, Point/Point→Undef, Point/Vector→Undef.
Add Point/Vector arms to eval_div using scale_components helper: (Point,scalar)→divide→Point, (Vector,scalar)→divide→Vector. Add pragmatic deviation comment for Point/scalar. Pass step-9 tests.
Write failing test for construct_point_or_vector with empty args (expected_n=0, args=[]): should return Undef, not panic.

Fix construct_point_or_vector in reify-stdlib: replace `args[0].dimension()` with `match args.first() { Some(v) => v.dimension(), None => return Value::Undef }` to prevent latent panic on empty args. Pass step-11 test.

Add stdlib tests exercising tensor_components_f64 with Value::Vector/Point inputs: magnitude(Value::Vector([3m,4m,0m]))≈5m, normalize(Value::Vector([3,4,0]))=[0.6,0.8,0], dot(Value::Vector,Value::Vector), cross(Value::Vector,Value::Vector). Verify these pass with existing impl (tensor_components_f64 already accepts Point/Vector).

Fix point_vector_semantic_distinction test in reify-stdlib: add content_hash assertions (p2.content_hash() != v2.content_hash()), and Display format assertions (format!("{}", p2).starts_with("point(") && format!("{}", v2).starts_with("vec(")). Replace dead `let _ = items` code.

design_decisions.decision: Point negation returns Undef, not a negated Point
design_decisions.rationale: Affine geometry: only vectors have negation (-v is the opposite direction). Negating a point has no valid meaning in affine space. This matches the existing Point+Point→Undef rule (spec 3.3.1). If upstream type checker blocks this, the eval-level check is defense-in-depth.

design_decisions.decision: Scalar * Point and Point / Scalar are intentionally allowed (pragmatic deviation from strict affine rules)
design_decisions.rationale: Needed for weighted interpolation, barycentric coordinates, and affine combinations. The type checker can enforce stricter rules upstream; the eval layer allows it as a pragmatic convenience. Document with inline comment.

design_decisions.decision: Point - Point produces Vector (displacement), not Undef
design_decisions.rationale: Standard affine geometry: the difference of two points is a displacement vector. This is mathematically valid and widely expected.
Subtracting a point from a vector has no geometric meaning in affine space. Falls through to catch-all arm naturally.

design_decisions.decision: Extract componentwise_binop and scale_components helpers to eliminate DRY violations
design_decisions.rationale: The collect→any(is_undef)→wrap pattern is repeated ~10 times across eval_add, eval_sub, eval_mul, eval_div, and neg_value for Tensor/Point/Vector. Extracting parameterized helpers eliminates duplication and ensures future edge-case fixes propagate to all variant handling.

design_decisions.decision: Skip componentwise_op early-exit optimization, arrayvec for tensor_components, and empty Point/Vector dimension fallback
design_decisions.rationale: Per escalation triage: early-exit is a separate optimization concern, arrayvec is premature for current scale, and DIMENSIONLESS fallback for empty containers is an acceptable convention.

reuse.how: Extract into componentwise_binop helper, then reuse for Point/Vector arms with different wrap constructors
Extract into scale_components helper, then reuse for Point/Vector arms in both eval_mul and eval_div
Pass Value::Point and Value::Vector as the wrap parameter to the extracted helpers
Follow same CompiledExpr::literal + CompiledExpr::binop/unop pattern but with Value::Point/Value::Vector instead of Value::Tensor
stdlib operations (magnitude, normalize, dot, cross) already work with Value::Point/Value::Vector inputs; step-13 just adds test coverage to verify

reuse.what: Display and content_hash already implemented for Point/Vector
reuse.how: Step-14 adds assertions to the existing semantic distinction test to verify these work correctly
